Adversarial verifier, HC-GRC agent/pipeline deep audit. Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc. REFUTE this candidate by reading the real code AND the cited spec. Remember: an intentional Phase-0/1 stub (NotImplementedError + IMPLEMENTED=False) is NOT a defect — set is_stub_by_design=true and confirmed=false for those. Default confirmed=false unless it is a genuine defect in implemented logic, a real research-integrity hole, or a real code-vs-spec contradiction.

CANDIDATE (unit xc-research-integrity):
title: HARKing prevention check in HypothesisFormalizerAgent is stubbed and never called
severity: high
location: src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer/agent.py:121-140
problem: HypothesisFormalizerAgent defines _validate_no_harking() (lines 121-140) with a docstring explaining HARKing detection. However: (1) the implementation is empty (line 140 just `pass`), (2) this method is never called anywhere in the codebase (no grep results), (3) the method accepts exploratory_p_values parameter but never uses it, (4) the docstring itself acknowledges 'This check cannot be fully automated — it is a process constraint documented here for explicitness', which means the implementation was abandoned as incomplete. Post-Gate-2 hypotheses added after viewing exploratory results are therefore undetected.
spec_or_invariant: docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md Section 12 'Exploratory-Guided Hypothesis Selection' (lines 190-202): 'Hypotheses that looked uninteresting in exploratory analysis are less likely to be registered, even if they would have been pre-specified before data was seen... Effect sizes for data-adaptive hypotheses are subject to inflation relative to theory-derived hypotheses.' Also agents/01-research/hypothesis-formalizer/AGENT.md Behavioral Constraints: 'Never use exploratory p-values to select which hypotheses to formalize (HARKing)'
proposed_fix: Implement a real HARKing detection layer: (1) at Gate 2, compare hypothesis_set against a pre-registered 'theory_derived_hypotheses' list (from methods_scaffolding pre-registration checkpoint), (2) mark each hypothesis as 'theory_derived', 'data_adaptive', or 'exploratory', (3) log all three categories to the PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER, (4) require Gate 2 human confirmation that 'I reviewed the data-adaptive hypotheses and confirm they were not selected based on observed p-values'. Add this confirmation to Gate 2 checklist line 276-283.

Verify against actual code + spec (read-only). Cite file:line and the exact spec clause. Be honest about severity.

1	"""
2	Hypothesis Formalizer Agent.
3	
4	PURPOSE:
5	Translates research questions and exploratory findings into formally specified
6	SAP-compatible hypotheses (H[module].[n] format). The formalized hypothesis
7	includes: the null, the alternative, the named test, the decision rule, and
8	the effect size threshold. Output is added to the SAP at Gate 2.
9	
10	PROTECTED AGENT: Prompts may not be modified autonomously by Agent Evolution.
11	Any modification requires Escalation approval (ADR-0015 §77).
12	
13	WORKFLOW POSITION:
14	  EDA findings → Hypothesis Formalizer → SAP (Gate 2 package)
15	  → Statistical Analyst (Phase 2 confirmatory tests)
16	
17	HYPOTHESIS FORMAT (H[module].[n]):
18	  module: 1=P1, 2=P2, 3=P3, 4=P4, 5=P5
19	  n: sequential integer within module, starting at 1
20	
21	GATE 2 PACKAGE:
22	  The Gate 2 proposal contains the formalized hypothesis set. The operator
23	  approves the hypothesis set as part of Gate 2. Any post-Gate-2 hypothesis
24	  addition requires a SAP amendment entry in the Preregistration Ledger.
25	
26	WHAT THIS AGENT MUST NOT DO:
27	  - Add hypotheses after viewing test data (SAP violation)
28	  - Use exploratory p-values to select which hypotheses to formalize (HARKing)
29	  - Formalize hypotheses after the data split is accessed (all must be pre-Gate-2)
30	  - Generate effect size thresholds from the test split (circular reasoning)
31	
32	See AGENT.md: agents/01-research/hypothesis-formalizer/AGENT.md
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from pathlib import Path
39	from typing import Any, Literal
40	
41	from ...state import HCGRCState
42	from ..base import BaseResearchAgent, SAPViolationError
43	
44	
45	@dataclass
46	class FormalHypothesis:
47	    """
48	    A single formalized hypothesis for the SAP.
49	
50	    All fields required before this hypothesis is added to the Gate 2 package.
51	    Field names match the Instructor schema enforced at the output boundary —
52	    see agents/01-research/hypothesis-formalizer/AGENT.md Skills section.
53	    """
54	    hypothesis_id: str                  # H[module].[n] — e.g., "H1.1"
55	    null_hypothesis: str                # H0: specific, falsifiable statement
56	    alternative_hypothesis: str         # H1: specific, falsifiable statement
57	    named_test: str                     # e.g., "Spearman rank correlation"
58	    decision_rule: str                  # criterion: "p < 0.05 and rho > 0.40"
59	    effect_size_metric: str             # e.g., "Spearman rho"
60	    effect_size_threshold: float        # minimum meaningful effect
61	    tail: Literal["one-tailed", "two-tailed"]
62	    module: str                         # analysis module — e.g., "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4", "P5"
63	    citation_anchors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # DOI/URL citations grounding the hypothesis
64	    alpha: float = 0.05
65	    power_target: float = 0.80
66	    comparison_family: str = ""         # SAP §6 family this test belongs to
67	    correction_method: str | None = None  # Bonferroni/BH/none — as specified in SAP §6
68	    rationale: str = ""                 # why this hypothesis, what it tests
69	
70	
71	class HypothesisFormalizerAgent(BaseResearchAgent):
72	    """
73	    Hypothesis formalizer — translates EDA findings to SAP hypotheses.
74	
75	    Runs only in exploratory phase (before Gate 2).
76	    Output is appended to the SAP and included in the Gate 2 package.
77	    """
78	
79	    PROTECTED = True
80	    AGENT_ID = "hypothesis-formalizer"
81	
82	    def __init__(
83	        self,
84	        sap_path: Path | str = Path("docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md"),
85	        ledger_path: Path | str = Path("docs/protocol/PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md"),
86	    ) -> None:
87	        self.sap_path = Path(sap_path)
88	        self.ledger_path = Path(ledger_path)
89	
90	    def run_exploratory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
91	        """
92	        Formalize hypotheses from EDA findings for Gate 2 package.
93	
94	        Must NOT use test data — all hypotheses formalized before Gate 2.
95	        Must NOT use p-values from exploratory analysis to select hypotheses (HARKing).
96	        Effect size thresholds must be grounded in domain knowledge or prior literature,
97	        not derived from the exploratory data.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError(
100	            "Hypothesis formalization not yet implemented. "
101	            "Phase 1 implementation will: (1) receive EDA summary from EDA agent, "
102	            "(2) formalize each research question into H[module].[n] format, "
103	            "(3) specify named test, decision rule, and effect size threshold, "
104	            "(4) append to SAP markdown, (5) return hypothesis list for Gate 2 package."
105	        )
106	
107	    def run_confirmatory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
108	        """
109	        Hypothesis Formalizer does not run in confirmatory phase.
110	
111	        Adding hypotheses after Gate 2 requires a SAP amendment — not this agent.
112	        Raises SAPViolationError.
113	        """
114	        raise SAPViolationError(
115	            "Hypothesis Formalizer may not add hypotheses after Gate 2. "
116	            "All pre-registered hypotheses must be formalized before Gate 2 approval. "
117	            "Post-Gate-2 additions require a LEDGER sap-amendment entry and explicit "
118	            "justification that this does not constitute HARKing."
119	        )
120	
121	    def _validate_no_harking(
122	        self,
123	        hypothesis: FormalHypothesis,
124	        exploratory_p_values: dict[str, float] | None = None,
125	    ) -> None:
126	        """
127	        Validate that hypothesis formalization is not driven by exploratory p-values.
128	
129	        HARKing (Hypothesizing After Results are Known) occurs when effect size
130	        thresholds or hypothesis direction are selected because they match the
131	        exploratory data. This check cannot be fully automated — it is a process
132	        constraint documented here for explicitness.
133	
134	        If exploratory_p_values are provided, log a warning if the hypothesis
135	        direction matches a significant exploratory finding — this is not a block
136	        but is flagged for Gate 2 human review.
137	        """
138	        if exploratory_p_values:
139	            # Log the association for Gate 2 reviewer — do not block
140	            pass

1	# Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP)
2

4	> This document is a template. The SAP is finalized at Gate 2, after exploratory characterization of the SCF corpus. Fields marked `[To be populated]` are structurally deferred — populating them before exploratory analysis runs would constitute pre-specification of effect sizes without empirical foundation.
5	>
6	> **Locked now (framework pre-registration):** Analytical workflow, hypothesis format, correction methods, split strategy (GroupKFold on SCF control IDs per issue #60), unit of analysis (SCF control, ~1,400 effective observations per issue #67), and model selection criteria (Phase 1/2 protocol per ADR-0013).
7	>
8	> **Deferred to Gate 2:** Specific hypotheses, effect size thresholds, primary outcome measures, multiple comparison families.
9	
10	**Version**: [version]  
11	**Date locked**: [date — this document is immutable after this date]  
12	**Pre-registration record**: `protocol/registration/`  
13	
14	This document specifies every analysis this project will conduct. It is written and committed before any confirmatory analysis runs. Analyses not specified here that are conducted after data examination are exploratory and must be labeled as such in all reporting.
15	
16	The SAP is a single document. It is not a collection of hypothesis files. Every analysis — primary, secondary, sensitivity, and exploratory — is listed here in one place so the full scope of the analytical program is visible and auditable.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 1. Primary Analysis
21	
22	The primary analysis is the one the project is fundamentally designed to answer. There is only one. If a project has multiple primary analyses, it has multiple projects.
23	
24	**Primary question**: [To be populated — maps to CRQ1]

30	**Decision rule**: The primary analysis is considered to support H1 if [specific criterion — effect size, p-value, CI — stated here].

34	## 2. Secondary Analyses
35	
36	Secondary analyses address additional questions that are pre-specified but not the primary focus. They are fully pre-registered but interpreted with the understanding that they were designed to explore, not definitively test.
37	
38	| Analysis ID | Question | Test | Outcome measure | Decision rule |
39	|-------------|---------|------|-----------------|---------------|
40	| SA-01 | | | | |
41	| SA-02 | | | | |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## 3. Sensitivity Analyses
46	
47	Sensitivity analyses test whether primary findings hold under alternative analytical choices. They are pre-specified here so they cannot be selectively reported.
48	
49	| Analysis ID | What varies | Baseline choice | Alternative choice | Expected direction of change |
50	|-------------|------------|-----------------|-------------------|------------------------------|
51	| SEN-01 | | | | |
52	
53	---

55	## 4. Exploratory Analyses
56	
57	Exploratory analyses are hypothesis-generating. They are conducted on the data and their findings are stated as hypotheses for future confirmatory work. They are NOT used to revise the conclusions of the confirmatory analyses above.
58	
59	[List exploratory analyses planned before data examination. Additional exploratory analyses discovered during analysis are logged in `lab_notebook.md` with timestamps.]
60	
61	---
62	
63	## 5. Hypothesis Register
64	
65	Every confirmatory hypothesis is listed here with its full specification. The pre-registration file in `protocol/registration/` records the timestamp.
66	
67	### Hypothesis Format
68	
69	```
70	ID:           H[module].[number]
71	Registered:   YYYY-MM-DD (commit hash: [hash])
72	Question:     [one sentence — specific, directional, falsifiable]
73	H1:           [what we expect to find]
74	H0:           [what the world looks like if H1 is false]
75	Test:         [named statistical test]
76	Assumptions:  [what must hold for this test to be valid, and how verified]
77	Effect size:  [measure and minimum meaningful magnitude]
78	α:            [significance threshold — typically 0.05]
79	Power:        [target — typically 0.80]
80	Required N:   [from power analysis]
81	Available N:  [actual]
82	Powered:      [yes / no — if no, analysis is exploratory]
83	Correction:   [multiple comparison method if part of a family]
84	Confounds:    [named confounds and how controlled]
85	Data:         [specific dataset and split]
86	Script:       [path to analysis script]
87	```
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 6. Multiple Comparisons
92	
93	This project conducts [N] confirmatory hypothesis tests. Multiple comparison correction is applied as follows:
94	
95	**Family definition**: [Which tests form a family — tests addressing the same research question are one family]
96	
97	**Correction method**: [Benjamini-Hochberg FDR for large families; Bonferroni for small families where FWER control is required]
98	
99	**Corrected thresholds**: [List adjusted α per family]
100	
101	Tests are grouped into families here before analysis begins. Grouping is not adjusted after results are seen.
102	
103	| Family | Hypotheses included | Correction method | Adjusted α |
104	|--------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|
105	| F1 | | | |

109	## 7. Data Splits
110	
111	**Unit of analysis**: SCF control (~1,400 unique controls). Not mapping rows (280,000). All statistical tests assume independence at the control level. Mixed-effects modeling is required if mapping-level analysis is conducted.
112	
113	**Split strategy**: GroupKFold on SCF control IDs. No SCF control text appears in both train and test partitions. The DataSplitAgent asserts `len(set(train_control_ids) ∩ test_control_ids) == 0` before writing partition assignments to provenance. This assertion is logged and is a required Gate 2 artifact.
114	
115	**Leakage analysis**: Entity-level leakage (same control text in train and test via different mapping rows) is controlled by the GroupKFold strategy above. Temporal leakage: N/A — SCF corpus is a static snapshot. Feature leakage: [To be populated at Gate 2 — specific features identified after EDA].
116	
117	| Split | Purpose | Size | Grouped by | Touched when |
118	|-------|---------|------|-----------|--------------|
119	| Training | Model fitting | ~70% | SCF control ID | Phase 1 only |
120	| Validation | Tuning and model selection | ~15% | SCF control ID | Phase 1 only |
121	| Test | Final confirmatory evaluation | ~15% | SCF control ID | Once, at Gate 2 confirmatory run |
122	
123	**Cross-validation**: GroupKFold, k=5, groups=scf_control_id, on training split only.
124	
125	**Calibration sample:** The Phase 2 calibration sample for model selection (ADR-0013) is the validation split (15% of SCF controls). No additional partition is created for model selection. The 70/15/15 split accounts for all data partitions.

129	## 8. Missing Data Protocol
130	
131	**Expected missingness**: [anticipated rate per variable]
132	
133	**Mechanism assumption**: [MCAR / MAR / MNAR — and how assessed]
134	
135	**Handling strategy**: [complete case analysis / imputation method — specified before seeing missing data pattern]
136

139	## 9. Baseline Models
140	
141	The following baselines are defined and run before any complex model is evaluated. Results are locked.
142	
143	| Baseline | Description | Metric | Result (filled after baseline run) |
144	|----------|-------------|--------|-----------------------------------|
145	| Trivial | [e.g., majority class / mean] | | |
146	| Simple | [e.g., TF-IDF cosine / logistic regression] | | |
147	
148	No model is reported as performing "well" unless it beats the simple baseline on the primary metric by the pre-specified margin.
149	
150	---
151	
152	## 10. Model Selection Criteria
153	
154	Primary embedding model selection follows the two-phase protocol from ADR-0013. The selection criterion is fixed here and is not changed after any SCF data is examined.
155	
156	**Candidate model set diversity requirement:**
157	The candidate set must include at minimum: one or more general-purpose models (all-MiniLM, BGE, E5 family) plus two diversity anchors — (1) a model domain fine-tuned on legal/regulatory text; (2) a model from a different architecture class (sparse retrieval or substantially different training corpus). T-01 identifies specific models during Phase 0. Diversity anchor selection logged to Preregistration Ledger before Phase 1 runs. Phase 1 does not run without both anchors present.
158	
159	**Phase 1 — Benchmark evaluation (no SCF data):**
160	Candidate models evaluated on: (a) STS-B and SICK-R (general-purpose, coarse filter only — see Section 12 for domain shift limitation); (b) any regulatory/legal domain STS benchmarks identified by T-01 during Phase 0 — if found, added; if not found, absence documented and Phase 2 weighted more heavily; (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs: similarity anchors = STRM '= Equal To' pairs; dissimilarity anchors = STRM '∅ No Relation' pairs across unrelated domains. Selection criterion: composite of Spearman correlation on benchmark sets and anchor consistency score. Produces ranked shortlist of 3–5 models.
161	
162	**Phase 2 — Calibration sample evaluation (validation split):**
163	Shortlisted models evaluated on the validation split (15% of SCF controls) using:
164	- Silhouette score on the SCF control embedding space
165	- Anchor consistency score: proportion of '= Equal To' anchor pairs ranked above '∅ No Relation' anchor pairs
166	- Cross-model agreement: pairwise Spearman between models' pairwise similarity rankings across the full candidate set including diversity anchors
167	
168	**Primary model**: Model with highest composite score across all three Phase 2 criteria. Designation logged to Preregistration Ledger with cryptographic timestamp before Gate 2 split executes.
169	
170	**All other models**: Designated as secondary/sensitivity analyses at the same timestamp. Model selection is not revisited after Gate 2.
171	
172	**OPEN DECISION — DIVERGENCE-01:** Operationalization of model-STRM divergence metric. Must be selected and logged to the Preregistration Ledger before any STRM comparison results are examined. Candidate approaches: (A) Map STRM categories to ordinal numeric scale + Spearman rank correlation; (B) Threshold model similarity scores into STRM categories + agreement rate; (C) Polychoric correlation treating STRM as ordinal; (D) Rank preservation test. Selection criterion: choose the operationalization that makes fewest assumptions about STRM category spacing given the observed label distribution from EDA. This is a hard Gate 2 prerequisite — STRM comparison results cannot be examined before DIVERGENCE-01 is closed.
173	
174	---
175	
176	## 11. Reporting Standards
177	
178	- Effect size and confidence interval reported for every primary and secondary result
179	- Confidence intervals: 95%, bootstrap (10,000 resamples) unless parametric assumptions are verified
180	- Null results reported with effect size estimate and CI — "no significant difference" without a CI is not a finding
181	- All figures include uncertainty visualization (error bars, CI ribbons, or equivalent)
182	- Subgroup analyses pre-specified in Section 2; post-hoc subgroup analyses labeled exploratory
183	
184	---
185	
186	## 12. Limitations and Design Tradeoffs
187	
188	This section is required. It acknowledges known limitations of the design before any results are seen. It is written here so it cannot be omitted or softened after results are known.
189	
190	### Exploratory-Guided Hypothesis Selection
191	
192	The SAP is finalized at Gate 2, after exploratory analysis. This is intentional — specifying effect sizes before seeing the data produces authorial guesses presented as pre-specified thresholds (see issue #59 resolution). But this design has a cost: hypotheses entering Gate 2 are selected based on what looked tractable or promising in the exploratory phase. This is not traditional pre-registration. It is a two-stage adaptive design with a registration checkpoint between stages.
193	
194	**Nature of the bias:** Effect sizes for data-adaptive hypotheses are subject to inflation relative to theory-derived hypotheses. Hypotheses that looked uninteresting in exploratory analysis are less likely to be registered, even if they would have been pre-specified before data was seen.
195	
196	**Mitigation 1 — Multiple testing correction:** Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction applied across all confirmatory hypotheses regardless of whether they are theory-derived or data-adaptive. Correction is applied to the full registered hypothesis set, not a subset selected after results.
197	
198	**Mitigation 2 — Mandatory null result reporting:** Every hypothesis that enters the confirmatory phase is reported in all outputs regardless of outcome. Selective reporting of significant findings is not permitted. The full hypothesis set and all test results appear in the Preregistration Ledger and in any published appendix.
199	
200	**Mitigation 3 — Epistemic status labeling:** All findings are labeled with their epistemic origin — `theory-derived` (pre-specified from literature/theory before any SCF data was seen), `data-adaptive` (registered before confirmatory test but generated from exploratory findings), or `exploratory` (not pre-registered). Labels appear in all outputs including publications. Readers assess inference strength for each finding independently.
201	
202	This limitation is disclosed in the methods section of all publications. The adaptive design is not a flaw to hide — it is the appropriate design for an autonomous iterative research system operating on data it has not yet seen. The mitigations above provide the transparency that makes adaptive findings interpretable.
203	
204	### Phase 1 Benchmark Domain Shift
205	
206	STS-B and SICK-R were built from everyday language corpora (image captions, news headlines). They do not test GRC-domain semantic distinctions. Phase 1 benchmark results are treated as a coarse filter only. Domain-appropriate evaluation relies on SCF structural metadata anchor pairs and Phase 2 calibration sample results. A human-labeled SCF control pair gold standard evaluation set does not currently exist and is designated as a post-publication community contribution milestone.
207	
208	### Anchor Consistency Circularity
209	
210	The anchor consistency score uses STRM-asserted relationship pairs ('= Equal To', '∅ No Relation') as weak supervision. STRM reliability is simultaneously under investigation as part of the secondary analysis. This creates a partial circularity: STRM assertions are used to validate the embedding while their reliability is being tested. The circularity is partial — silhouette score and cross-model agreement are independent of STRM — and is disclosed here prior to any results being examined.
211	
212	### STRM Inter-Rater Reliability
213	
214	STRM inter-rater reliability (Fleiss kappa on a re-rated sample) requires GRC domain expert raters. Two paths are available: (1) rater recruitment during Phase 0 — if selected, documented as a Gate 1 prerequisite; (2) if rater recruitment is infeasible, STRM reliability is unestimated. In path 2, the interpretation requiring evidence of STRM reliability is unavailable and model-STRM divergence cannot be attributed to annotator inconsistency vs. systematic annotation error. Path selection is logged to the Preregistration Ledger before Gate 1. Rater recruitment is also designated as a post-publication community contribution milestone.

1	---
2	name: hypothesis-formalizer
3	description: Converts candidate research directions from the Ideation Agent into precisely stated, testable hypotheses conforming to SAP schema requirements. Each hypothesis is assigned a unique ID, linked to a statistical test, given a pre-specified effect size threshold, and registered in the SAP before any data is touched. The formal record that separates confirmatory from exploratory claims.
4	version: 1.0.0
5	team: 01-research
6	status: primary
7	trigger: always
8	author: HC-GRC
9	tags: [Hypothesis Formalization, SAP, Pre-registration, DSPy, Instructor]
10	skills: [dspy, instructor]
11	tools: [mcp-sap-validator, mcp-lab-notebook]
12	---
13	
14	# Hypothesis Formalizer
15	
16	## Purpose
17	
18	A research direction becomes a hypothesis the moment it is stated precisely enough to be falsified. Before that moment it is a hope. The Hypothesis Formalizer makes that transition explicit and irreversible — it converts a direction into a formal claim with a unique ID, a specified null hypothesis, a named statistical test, a pre-specified minimum effect size, and a registered entry in the SAP. Once registered, the hypothesis cannot be modified without a logged amendment. This is the mechanism that prevents post-hoc hypothesis adjustment — the single most common source of false positives in empirical research.
19	
20	## Position in Workflow
21	
22	Runs after Ideation Agent and human Gate 1 selection. Outputs must be locked in the SAP before the Data Agent runs train/test splits. Nothing downstream touches test-set data until this agent has completed and Gate 2 is confirmed.
23	
24	```
25	[Ideation Agent: candidate directions] → human Gate 1 selection
26	        ↓
27	[Hypothesis Formalizer] → registered hypotheses in SAP
28	        ↓
29	Human Gate 2: SAP review + pre-registration lock
30	        ↓
31	[Data Agent: run train/test split]
32	```
33	
34	## Inputs
35	
36	| Input | Source | Format | Schema / Notes |
37	|-------|--------|--------|----------------|
38	| Selected research directions | Human (Gate 1) | Pydantic SelectedDirections | Human-approved subset of Ideation Agent output |
39	| Literature gap + citations | Literature Agent | Pydantic LiteratureGap | Citation anchors for each hypothesis |
40	| SAP schema | docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md | Markdown + JSON schema | Format requirements for hypothesis registration |
41	| Theoretical framework | docs/protocol/01_theoretical_framework.md | Markdown | Construct definitions that govern operationalization |
42	
43	## Outputs / Artifacts
44	
45	| Artifact | Destination | Format | Schema / Notes |
46	|----------|-------------|--------|----------------|
47	| Formal hypothesis register | docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md | Markdown table | H[module].[number]: null hypothesis, alternative, test, effect size threshold, alpha, power |
48	| Pydantic hypothesis models | SAP validator store | JSON | Machine-readable, used by Code Review Agent to validate script headers |
49	| Lab notebook entry | Provenance Agent | Markdown | All directions considered, which were formalized and which were deferred, with rationale |
50	
51	## Tools & MCP Servers
52	
53	| Tool | Purpose | MCP Server | Notes |
54	|------|---------|-----------|-------|
55	| SAP validator | Confirm each hypothesis entry meets schema before registration | mcp-sap-validator | Rejects malformed entries — must pass before SAP is updated |
56	| Lab notebook | Record formalization decisions and deferred hypotheses | mcp-lab-notebook | Append-only |
57	
58	## Skills Used
59	
60	- **DSPy** — Optimizes the formalization prompts. Given a research direction, DSPy-optimized prompts reliably produce well-formed null/alternative hypothesis pairs, correctly scoped to the available data and analysis methods.
61	- **Instructor** — All hypothesis outputs are typed Pydantic models. Instructor enforces that every hypothesis has: hypothesis_id, null_hypothesis, alternative_hypothesis, named_test, decision_rule, effect_size_metric, effect_size_threshold, tail, module, citation_anchors, alpha, power_target. No partial hypothesis objects leave this agent.
62	
63	## Handoffs
64	
65	**Receives from**: Ideation Agent (candidates), Human Gate 1 (selection), Literature Agent (citations)
66	**Passes to**: SAP (hypothesis register), Code Review Agent (hypothesis IDs for script header validation), Statistical Analyst (test specifications)
67	**Human gate**: Gate 2 — human reviews the complete hypothesis register, confirms each hypothesis is testable, and approves the SAP for pre-registration lock. No test-set data is accessed before this gate.
68	
69	## Behavioral Constraints
70	- **Protected research agent:** This agent's prompts may not be modified autonomously by Agent Evolution. Any prompt modification requires Escalation approval before taking effect. Modifying this agent mid-tier against SCF corpus data constitutes a research design change that bypasses Gate 2 (per ADR-0015, #77).
71	
72	- Never register a hypothesis without a named statistical test and pre-specified effect size threshold — vague hypotheses do not enter the SAP.
73	- Never modify a registered hypothesis after Gate 2 without logging a formal amendment with: amendment ID, original text, revised text, reason, date, and whether the change affects the analysis plan.
74	- Never generate a hypothesis that refers to test-set observations — hypotheses are pre-specified before the data split runs.
75	- Never register more than one primary hypothesis per module without documenting the multiple comparisons correction method (Bonferroni, Benjamini-Hochberg) in the SAP.
76	- Exploratory directions that do not meet formalization standards are logged as EDA questions, not deferred hypotheses — they cannot be upgraded to confirmatory status without a new pre-registration.
77	
78	## Failure Modes & Recovery
79	
80	| Failure | Detection | Recovery |
81	|---------|-----------|----------|
82	| Direction too vague to formalize | DSPy produces null hypothesis with > 3 qualifications | Return to Ideation Agent with specificity constraints; log attempt |
83	| SAP schema validation fails | mcp-sap-validator returns error | Fix schema error; re-submit; never bypass validator |
84	| Hypothesis conflicts with existing SAP entry | Duplicate detection on null_hypothesis text | Flag conflict; do not register duplicate; escalate to human |
85	| No statistical test exists for hypothesis | Statistical Analyst confirms no applicable test | Downgrade to exploratory question; log reason; notify human |
86	
87	## Evaluation Criteria
88	
89	- [ ] Every registered hypothesis has all required SAP fields — zero partial registrations
90	- [ ] All hypotheses traceable to a literature citation and a Gate 1 human-approved direction
91	- [ ] Multiple comparisons correction documented for any module with > 1 primary hypothesis
92	- [ ] Gate 2 timestamp recorded in lab notebook before any downstream data split
93	- [ ] Amendment log empty at Gate 2 (all formalization happens before lock, not after)
94	
95	## Notes
96	
97	The Hypothesis Formalizer is the last agent that can write to the primary SAP hypothesis register before Gate 2 lock. After Gate 2, the register is read-only except through the formal amendment process. The Code Review Agent enforces this at every confirmatory script commit — if a script header references an unregistered hypothesis ID, the commit is rejected.
98

1	"""
2	Gate nodes for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	Five gates enforce human approval at structural transition points.
5	Each gate issues a LangGraph interrupt(), surfacing a structured proposal
6	to the operator. The operator responds via Claude on any device (ADR-0014).
7	
8	Gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator_node — never directly here.
9	On rejection, each gate appends a failure_event for Agent Evolution monitoring.
10	
11	Gate definitions:
12	  Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint (Phase 0 complete, all prerequisites verified)
13	  Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory (hypothesis lock, test data unlock)
14	  Gate 3 — Finding significance review (before publication pipeline)
15	  Gate 4 — Manuscript submission approval
16	  Gate 5 — Escalation resolution gate (triggered by ADR-0014 Escalation loop)
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime
22	from typing import Any
23	
24	from langgraph.types import interrupt
25	
26	from ..state import HCGRCState
27	
28	# ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	
31	def _build_gate_proposal(gate_id: str, title: str, summary: str,
32	                          checklist: list[str], run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
33	    """Build the structured proposal surfaced to the operator via interrupt()."""
34	    return {
35	        "gate_id": gate_id,
36	        "title": title,
37	        "summary": summary,
38	        "checklist": checklist,
39	        "run_id": run_id,
40	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
41	        "instructions": (
42	            "Respond with: {'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', "
43	            "'rationale': '<your reasoning>'}"
44	        ),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	def _rejection_event(gate_id: str, rationale: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
49	    """Failure event appended to state for Agent Evolution monitoring."""
50	    return {
51	        "event_type": "gate_rejection",
52	        "gate_id": gate_id,
53	        "rationale": rationale,
54	        "run_id": run_id,
55	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
56	    }
57	
58	
59	_VALID_DECISIONS = {"approved", "rejected", "deferred"}
60	# Only 'approved' is terminal — it stops the gate re-firing on re-entry (don't
61	# re-unlock). 'rejected' routes back for re-review and 'deferred' parks the run for
62	# the operator to re-trigger later; BOTH must re-fire the interrupt when the gate
63	# is re-entered, so neither is terminal (pass-3 #5).
64	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = {"approved"}
65	
66	
67	def _parse_operator_response(resp: Any, gate_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
68	    """
69	    Validate the operator's interrupt() response and return (decision, rationale).
70	
71	    A malformed response must NOT be silently coerced to 'rejected' (that would
72	    let a non-dict or a typo'd decision masquerade as a real governance decision,
73	    #163/#180). Fail loud so the operator re-submits a well-formed response on the
74	    next resume.
75	    """
76	    if not isinstance(resp, dict):
77	        raise ValueError(
78	            f"{gate_id}: operator response must be a dict like "
79	            f"{{'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', 'rationale': '...'}}, "
80	            f"got {type(resp).__name__}."
81	        )
82	    decision = resp.get("decision")
83	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
84	        raise ValueError(
85	            f"{gate_id}: operator response 'decision' must be one of "
86	            f"{sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)}, got {decision!r}."
87	        )
88	    rationale = resp.get("rationale") or "No rationale provided."
89	    return decision, rationale
90	
91	
92	def _already_decided(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str) -> bool:
93	    """
94	    True if this gate already holds a TERMINAL decision (approved only).
95	
96	    Guards against a re-invocation re-firing the interrupt and re-running
97	    downstream effects on top of a terminal decision (#177). A 'rejected' record
98	    is deliberately NOT terminal: the Gate-2 reject → revise → re-review loop
99	    (route_after_gate_2 → exploratory → … → gate_2) must re-fire the interrupt so
100	    the operator is re-prompted, otherwise the gate short-circuits to {} and the
101	    graph spins until GraphRecursionError (pass-2 #184/#187). The loop-back path
102	    is responsible for producing the revised state that warrants re-review.
103	    """
104	    record = state.get("gate_status", {}).get(gate_id)
105	    return bool(record) and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS
106	
107	
108	def _finalize_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, decision: str,
109	                   rationale: str, reviewer: str = "operator") -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    """Build the gate node's state update: failure event (if not approved) + the
111	    authoritative gate_status record (written only by gate_coordinator_node)."""
112	    update: dict[str, Any] = {}
113	    if decision == "rejected":
114	        # 'deferred' is a park, not a failure — only a rejection is a failure_event
115	        # (a deferred decision recorded as a rejection mislabels the audit trail).
116	        update["failure_events"] = [_rejection_event(gate_id, rationale, state["run_id"])]
117	    from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
118	    update.update(gate_coordinator_node(state, gate_id, decision, rationale, reviewer=reviewer))
119	    return update
120	
121	
122	def _run_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, proposal: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	    """
124	    Drive one gate: idempotency guard, then interrupt() the operator and finalize.
125	
126	    Validation runs INSIDE a re-prompt loop (pass-3 #3): interrupt() consumes and
127	    persists the resume value before validation, so raising on a malformed value
128	    would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever. Instead, a malformed
129	    or mis-correlated response re-fires a fresh interrupt (next resume index)
130	    carrying the error, so the operator can re-submit and actually recover.
131	
132	    Gate-id correlation (pass-3 #4): if the operator response names a gate, it must
133	    match this gate — a stale/misrouted decision cannot be applied to the wrong gate.
134	    """
135	    if _already_decided(state, gate_id):
136	        return {}
137	    current = proposal
138	    while True:
139	        resp = interrupt(current)
140	        if isinstance(resp, dict) and resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id):
141	            current = {**proposal, "error": (
142	                f"response gate_id {resp.get('gate_id')!r} does not match {gate_id}")}
143	            continue
144	        try:
145	            decision, rationale = _parse_operator_response(resp, gate_id)
146	        except ValueError as exc:
147	            current = {**proposal, "error": str(exc)}
148	            continue
149	        return _finalize_gate(state, gate_id, decision, rationale)
150	
151	
152	# ── Gate 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	
155	def gate_1_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
156	    """
157	    Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint.
158	
159	    Fires before any SCF data enters the system. The operator verifies that
160	    all Phase 0 prerequisites are complete (ADR-0015, #71):
161	
162	      1. LangGraph skeleton running
163	      2. Gate 1 fires with synthetic payload (this test)
164	      3. Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed reproducible)
165	      4. PostgresSaver benchmark complete (throughput on target hardware)
166	      5. Governance dry-run complete (full escalation loop exercised)
167	      6. Diversity anchor models identified (T-01, ADR-0013)
168	
169	    The interrupt() surfaces the proposal and parks the graph until the
170	    operator responds. No SCF data is loaded before this gate approves.
171	    """
172	    checklist = [
173	        "LangGraph skeleton compiles and graph executes on synthetic payload",
174	        "Gate 1 interrupt fires and resolves correctly",
175	        "compute_data_split() produces identical splits on two independent runs (SHA-256 seed)",
176	        "PostgresSaver write/read benchmark complete — throughput logged to MLflow",
177	        "Governance dry-run complete — escalation loop exercised end-to-end",
178	        # ── Preregistration Ledger entries (logged before Phase 1 data ingestion) ──
179	        "LEDGER-0002: Diversity anchor model candidates logged (T-01 — 3 general + 2 anchors)",
180	        "LEDGER-0003: Fleiss kappa path selection logged (path 1: recruit raters | path 2: acknowledge unestimated)",
181	        # ── NOTE: DIVERGENCE-01 is a Gate 2 prerequisite, NOT Gate 1 ──────────────
182	        # DIVERGENCE-01 (operationalization of model-STRM divergence) requires the
183	        # STRM label distribution from EDA — which has not run yet at Gate 1.
184	        # Decision framework is defined in SAP §10. Specific operationalization is
185	        # selected post-EDA, logged to Preregistration Ledger before Gate 2.
186	        # Confirmed: SAP §10 DIVERGENCE-01 decision framework understood and acknowledged",
187	    ]
188	
189	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
190	        gate_id="gate_1",
191	        title="Gate 1 — Pre-Data Checkpoint",
192	        summary=(
193	            "Platform has completed Phase 0 verification. All structural prerequisites "
194	            "are in place before SCF corpus data enters the system. "
195	            "Approve to unlock data ingestion and begin Phase 1."
196	        ),
197	        checklist=checklist,
198	        run_id=state["run_id"],
199	    )
200	
201	    # interrupt() parks the graph and surfaces proposal to operator.
202	    # The operator's response dict is the return value of interrupt().
203	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_1", proposal)
204	
205	
206	# ── Gate 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	
209	def gate_2_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	    """
211	    Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory transition.
212	
213	    The most consequential gate. The operator reviews the pre-registered
214	    hypothesis set and approves unlocking the test split. Uses ADR-0014
215	    Escalation infrastructure (GitHub issue → Claude → decision record).
216	
217	    Hard prerequisites (checked before interrupt fires):
218	      - DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization logged to Preregistration Ledger
219	      - Primary embedding model designated and logged
220	      - Hypothesis set complete and committed to PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md
221	      - Data split idempotency verified (data_split_verified == True)
222	    """
223	    # Verify hard prerequisites before surfacing this irreversible gate. Gate 2
224	    # unlocks the test split, so the machine-checkable preconditions for a sound
225	    # exploratory→confirmatory transition are ALL enforced here, not just the data
226	    # split (pass-3 #1): exploratory analysis must be complete and a non-empty
227	    # pre-registered hypothesis set must exist before the operator can even be asked.
228	    hard_failures = []
229	    if not state.get("data_split_verified"):
230	        hard_failures.append("data_split_verified is False — run compute_data_split() first")
231	    if not state.get("exploratory_complete"):
232	        hard_failures.append(
233	            "exploratory_complete is False — P1-P5 exploratory analysis has not finished")
234	    if not state.get("hypothesis_set"):
235	        hard_failures.append(
236	            "hypothesis_set is empty — no pre-registered hypotheses to lock at Gate 2")
237	
238	    if hard_failures:
239	        # Write an authoritative gate_status record so the router and operator see
240	        # the failure (#164/#178). Decision is DEFERRED, not rejected: a system
241	        # prerequisite failure must PARK the run (route_after_gate_2 deferred→END),
242	        # not feed it into the operator reject→revise→re-review loop, which would
243	        # spin forever because re-running exploratory cannot satisfy an unmet
244	        # prerequisite (pass-3 #20). The operator re-triggers once prereqs are met.
245	        rationale = "Hard prerequisite failure: " + "; ".join(hard_failures)
246	        # 'deferred' is non-terminal, so this path re-runs whenever gate_2 is
247	        # re-entered (operator re-trigger after the park, or the reject→exploratory
248	        # →…→gate_2 loop) while prereqs are still unmet. failure_events has a plain
249	        # append reducer (state.py:86), so re-appending an identical
250	        # gate_prerequisite_failure on every re-entry duplicates the audit record
251	        # and biases the Agent Evolution failure-count signal. Only append when this
252	        # exact deferred prerequisite failure is not already recorded (#267).
253	        prior = state.get("gate_status", {}).get("gate_2") or {}
254	        already_recorded = (
255	            prior.get("decision") == "deferred" and prior.get("rationale") == rationale
256	        )
257	        update: dict[str, Any] = {}
258	        if not already_recorded:
259	            update["failure_events"] = [
260	                {
261	                    "event_type": "gate_prerequisite_failure",
262	                    "gate_id": "gate_2",
263	                    "failures": hard_failures,
264	                    "run_id": state["run_id"],
265	                    "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
266	                }
267	            ]
268	        from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
269	        update.update(
270	            gate_coordinator_node(state, "gate_2", "deferred", rationale, reviewer="system")
271	        )
272	        return update
273	
274	    checklist = [
275	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization selected and logged to Preregistration Ledger",
276	        "Primary embedding model designated and logged to Preregistration Ledger",
277	        "All secondary/sensitivity models designated at same timestamp",
278	        "Hypothesis set complete — every confirmatory hypothesis in PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md",
279	        "Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed, two independent runs identical)",
280	        "Exploratory analysis complete — no confirmatory tests have run",
281	        "SAP Section 1 (primary analysis) populated with specific hypothesis and effect size",
282	        "Multiple comparison families defined in SAP Section 6",
283	    ]
284	
285	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
286	        gate_id="gate_2",
287	        title="Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory Transition",
288	        summary=(
289	            "Exploratory analysis is complete. Hypotheses are pre-registered. "
290	            "Approving this gate unlocks the test split for confirmatory analysis. "
291	            "This action is irreversible — test data cannot be re-locked after Gate 2 approves."
292	        ),
293	        checklist=checklist,
294	        run_id=state["run_id"],
295	    )
296	
297	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_2", proposal)
298	
299	
300	# ── Gate 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
301	
302	
303	def gate_3_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
304	    """
305	    Gate 3 — EDA findings review before confirmatory phase begins.
306	
307	    Fires after HypothesisFormalizerAgent completes. The operator reviews
308	    P1-P5 exploratory outputs and the formalized hypothesis set before any
309	    confirmatory test runs. Approving this gate:
310	      - Unlocks the test split (in addition to Gate 2 — DataStewardAgent
311	        requires BOTH gate_2 AND gate_3 approved before test split access)
312	      - Confirms that DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization is final
313	      - Confirms the hypothesis set is complete for pre-registration
314	
315	    Hard prerequisite: exploratory_complete must be True (or all agents
316	    in stub_pending status is acceptable for a governance dry-run).
317	    """
318	    checklist = [
319	        # ── P1-P5 artifact presence ────────────────────────────────────────────
320	        "P1 (STRM NLP): EXP_P1_similarity.parquet and EXP_P1_disagreements.md present and reviewed",
321	        "P1: Model F1 scores on STRM classification recorded for all 5 LEDGER-0002 candidates",
322	        "P2 (Control Topology): EXP_P2_graph.graphml, topology.parquet, communities.parquet reviewed",
323	        "P2: NIST cluster constraint verified — NIST 800-53, CSF, 800-171 treated as ONE "
324	        "evidence source (not 3 independent NIST findings)",
325	        "P3 (Regulatory Convergence): EXP_P3_convergence.parquet and atlas.json reviewed — "
326	        "ConvergenceType distribution examined",
327	        "P4 (Risk Blindspot): EXP_P4_blindspots.json reviewed — false coverage cases "
328	        "excluded (controls without STRM mappings excluded from blindspot claims)",
329	        "P5 (AI Governance): EXP_P5_taxonomy_comparison.md reviewed — UMAP parameters "
330	        "confirmed consistent with SAP pre-registration",
331	        # ── Model designation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
332	        "Primary embedding model designated from LEDGER-0002 candidates based on Phase 1 F1 scores",
333	        "Primary model designation and all secondary/sensitivity models logged to "
334	        "Preregistration Ledger before this gate approves",
335	        "If best model F1 < 0.70: domain fine-tuning triggered via Team 06 and approved "
336	        "before proceeding — do not approve Gate 3 with an F1 below threshold",
337	        # ── SAP lock prerequisites ─────────────────────────────────────────────
338	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization finalized and logged to Preregistration Ledger — "
339	        "this decision requires STRM label distribution from EDA (now available)",
340	        "Hypothesis set reviewed — all H[module].[n] IDs assigned with null hypothesis, "
341	        "named test, effect size threshold, and decision rule",
342	        "Effect size thresholds are grounded in domain literature — not selected to "
343	        "match the EDA distribution (that would be HARKing)",
344	        "HARKing check: no hypothesis was added to the set after P1-P5 outputs were reviewed "
345	        "unless it was pre-specified in methods scaffolding",
346	        # ── Test split integrity ───────────────────────────────────────────────
347	        "No analysis agent has received test split paths — all EDA artifacts reference "
348	        "train/val splits only. Test split data_steward access log shows zero test reads",
349	        "Data split seed hash unchanged since Gate 2",
350	        # ── License compliance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
351	        "No SCF-derived embeddings, derived datasets, or analysis results have left the machine",
352	    ]
353	
354	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
355	        gate_id="gate_3",
356	        title="Gate 3 — EDA Findings Review",
357	        summary=(
358	            "Exploratory analysis is complete. P1-P5 have produced their EXP_ artifact sets. "
359	            "Hypotheses are formalized. Approving this gate unlocks the test split and "
360	            "confirms the hypothesis set for pre-registration. "
361	            "This is the last human checkpoint before any confirmatory tests run."
362	        ),
363	        checklist=checklist,
364	        run_id=state["run_id"],
365	    )
366	
367	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_3", proposal)
368	
369	
370	# ── Gate 4 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
371	
372	
373	def gate_4_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
374	    """
375	    Gate 4 — Confirmatory results review before reporting begins.
376	
377	    Fires after all confirmatory analyses complete (P1-P5 confirmatory runs +
378	    StatisticalAnalystAgent). The operator reviews statistical results before
379	    any output enters the reporting or dissemination pipeline.
380	
381	    Approving this gate:
382	      - Authorizes Repo Documentation Agent to write executive-mode finding summaries
383	      - Triggers the Branding Compliance Agent review for any executive content
384	      - Unlocks the Reporter Agent to begin manuscript preparation
385	
386	    This gate prevents premature disclosure of unreviewed statistical results.
387	    """
388	    checklist = [
389	        # ── Confirmatory analysis completeness ────────────────────────────────
390	        "All pre-registered H[module].[n] hypotheses have decision records: "
391	        "'reject null' or 'fail to reject null' — no hypothesis left undecided",
392	        "StatisticalAnalystAgent has produced ConfirmatoryResult records for all hypotheses — "
393	        "p_value, effect_size, ci_lower, ci_upper, n_obs, corrected_p_value present",
394	        # ── Statistical rigor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
395	        "Multiple comparison correction applied per SAP Section 6 — corrected_p_value "
396	        "values present and correction method matches SAP specification",
397	        "Effect sizes with confidence intervals reported for all significant findings",
398	        "Pre-specified sensitivity analyses complete and results recorded",
399	        "No post-hoc test additions — all tests match the pre-registered SAP exactly. "
400	        "Any deviation is a SAP amendment requiring separate LEDGER entry",
401	        # ── Null result integrity ──────────────────────────────────────────────
402	        "Null results documented with same level of detail as significant results — "
403	        "failing to reject null is a finding, not a non-finding",
404	        "Selective reporting check: number of decision records == number of "
405	        "pre-registered hypotheses (no cherry-picking)",
406	        # ── Methodological constraints ─────────────────────────────────────────
407	        "NIST cluster constraint respected in all findings — NIST 800-53, CSF, "
408	        "800-171 share one authorship source (not counted as independent validation)",
409	        "STRM reliability limitation disclosed in all finding summaries — "
410	        "per LEDGER-0003 (Path 2): interpretation pathway 'annotator inconsistency' "
411	        "is UNAVAILABLE; only 'quantified divergence exists' interpretation is available",
412	        "DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization matches the version logged to Preregistration Ledger",
413	        # ── Data and license compliance ────────────────────────────────────────
414	        "No SCF-derived data in any external-facing artifact (CC BY-ND 4.0)",
415	        "All statistical outputs reference data/splits/test/ DVC hash — "
416	        "confirms correct test set was used (seed_hash unchanged from Gate 2)",
417	        # ── Documentation triggers ─────────────────────────────────────────────
418	        "Orchestrator has triggered Repo Documentation Agent for finding documentation "
419	        "and executive summary preparation",
420	        "Branding Compliance Agent review queued for any executive-mode content",
421	    ]
422	
423	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
424	        gate_id="gate_4",
425	        title="Gate 4 — Confirmatory Results Review",
426	        summary=(
427	            "Confirmatory analysis is complete. All pre-registered hypotheses have decisions. "
428	            "Statistical results are ready for operator review before entering the reporting pipeline. "
429	            "Approving this gate authorizes executive-mode documentation and manuscript preparation."
430	        ),
431	        checklist=checklist,
432	        run_id=state["run_id"],
433	    )
434	
435	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_4", proposal)
436	
437	
438	def gate_5_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
439	    """Gate 5 — Escalation resolution (ADR-0014 Escalation loop)."""
440	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
441	        gate_id="gate_5",
442	        title="Gate 5 — Escalation Resolution",
443	        summary=(
444	            "Platform has filed an Escalation proposal. Operator review required before "
445	            "proceeding into new territory (new data sources, substantially different methods, "
446	            "expanded scope). Platform will not auto-proceed."
447	        ),
448	        checklist=[
449	            "Review Escalation proposal in GitHub issue",
450	            "Evaluate scope expansion request against project charter",
451	            "Approve, reject, or defer the proposal",
452	        ],
453	        run_id=state["run_id"],
454	    )
455	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_5", proposal)

1	"""
2	Phase 1 LangGraph node wrappers.
3	
4	Topology (Phase 1 exploratory subgraph):
5	
6	  phase_1_entry
7	      ↓
8	  data_pipeline          ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
9	      ↓                    → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent (sequential)
10	  exploratory_phase      ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential now;
11	      ↓                    TODO: parallelize via Send() when implementations live)
12	  hypothesis_formalize   ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
13	      ↓
14	  gate_2                 ← (in gates.py)
15	      ↓
16	  [approved: confirmatory_entry | rejected: exploratory_phase loop]
17	
18	Parallelization note:
19	  P1-P5 are currently called sequentially within exploratory_phase_node.
20	  When implementations are live, replace with Send()-based fan-out:
21	    def dispatch_exploratory(state) -> list[Send]:
22	        return [Send("p1_node", state), Send("p2_node", state), ...]
23	  Each worker appends to eda_artifacts and eda_agent_statuses via append reducers.
24	  After all workers complete, route to hypothesis_formalize.
25	  See LangGraph map-reduce pattern for the fan-in wiring.
26	
27	NotImplementedError handling:
28	  Each P1-P5 call is wrapped in try/except NotImplementedError. Agents that
29	  have not been implemented yet record a "stub_pending" status to eda_agent_statuses.
30	  This allows the graph to execute in tests and governance dry-runs without
31	  requiring all research implementations to be live.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import datetime
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	from ..agents import (
40	    DataAcquisitionAgent,
41	    DataCurationAgent,
42	    DataStewardAgent,
43	    EmbeddingAgent,
44	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent,
45	    P1StrmNlpAgent,
46	    P2ControlTopologyAgent,
47	    P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent,
48	    P4RiskBlindspotAgent,
49	    P5AiGovernanceAgent,
50	)
51	from ..state import HCGRCState
52	
53	# ── Agent singletons ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	# Created once at module load; nodes call instance methods.
55	
56	_data_acquisition = DataAcquisitionAgent()
57	_data_curation = DataCurationAgent()
58	_data_steward = DataStewardAgent()
59	_embedding_agent = EmbeddingAgent()
60	
61	_p1 = P1StrmNlpAgent()
62	_p2 = P2ControlTopologyAgent()
63	_p3 = P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent()
64	_p4 = P4RiskBlindspotAgent()
65	_p5 = P5AiGovernanceAgent()
66	_hypothesis_formalizer = HypothesisFormalizerAgent()
67	
68	
69	# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	
71	
72	def _utc_now() -> str:
73	    return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat()
74	
75	
76	def _stub_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
77	    """Status record written when an agent has not been implemented yet."""
78	    return {
79	        "agent_id": agent_id,
80	        "status": "stub_pending",
81	        "note": (
82	            f"{agent_id} implementation pending — NotImplementedError caught. "
83	            "Will run when Phase 1 is live on Mac mini."
84	        ),
85	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
86	        "run_id": run_id,
87	    }
88	
89	
90	def _failed_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
91	    """Status record written when an agent raises a real runtime error (not a stub).
92	
93	    Produces the 'failed' status the state schema documents, so one agent's bug is
94	    recorded and isolated instead of crashing the whole subgraph (#148)."""
95	    return {
96	        "agent_id": agent_id,
97	        "status": "failed",
98	        "error": repr(exc),
99	        "note": f"{agent_id} raised {type(exc).__name__} during execution",
100	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
101	        "run_id": run_id,
102	    }
103	
104	
105	def _failure_event(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	    """Failure event for Agent-Evolution / operator monitoring (append reducer)."""
107	    return {
108	        "event_type": "agent_runtime_error",
109	        "agent_id": agent_id,
110	        "error": repr(exc),
111	        "run_id": run_id,
112	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def _prov(activity: str, run_id: str, **kwargs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
117	    """Minimal PROV-DM compatible activity record."""
118	    return {
119	        "activity": activity,
120	        "run_id": run_id,
121	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
122	        **kwargs,
123	    }
124	
125	
126	# ── Data pipeline node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	
129	def data_pipeline_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
130	    """
131	    Orchestrates the sequential data pipeline:
132	      DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent
133	
134	    Each stage is blocked on its predecessor completing successfully. If any stage
135	    raises NotImplementedError (Phase 1 stub), the pipeline records a stub_pending
136	    status and returns with phase_1_ready=False.
137	
138	    When all four stages complete: sets phase_1_ready=True in state.
139	    """
140	    run_id = state["run_id"]
141	    pipeline_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
142	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
143	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
144	
145	    stages = [
146	        (_data_acquisition, "data-acquisition"),
147	        (_data_curation, "data-curation"),
148	        (_data_steward, "data-steward"),
149	        (_embedding_agent, "embedding-agent"),
150	    ]
151	
152	    accumulated_update: dict[str, Any] = {}
153	
154	    for agent, agent_id in stages:
155	        try:
156	            stage_update = agent.run(state)
157	            pipeline_statuses.append({
158	                "agent_id": agent_id,
159	                "status": "completed",
160	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
161	                "run_id": run_id,
162	            })
163	            # Merge stage update into accumulated (agent writes prov_activities etc.)
164	            for k, v in stage_update.items():
165	                if k == "prov_activities" and isinstance(v, list):
166	                    prov.extend(v)
167	                else:
168	                    accumulated_update[k] = v
169	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
170	            # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError.
171	            # Once IMPLEMENTED, a NotImplementedError is a real bug, not a stub (#202).
172	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
173	                pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
174	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
175	                prov.append(_prov(
176	                    f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
177	                ))
178	                break
179	            pipeline_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
180	            # Cannot continue pipeline if a stage is not implemented
181	            # Phase 1 is not ready until all four stages complete
182	            prov.append(_prov(
183	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
184	                run_id,
185	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
186	            ))
187	            break
188	        except Exception as exc:
189	            # A real runtime error in one stage is recorded as 'failed' and halts
190	            # the pipeline (later stages depend on earlier ones) without crashing
191	            # the graph or discarding prior stages' output (#148). A governance
192	            # violation must propagate — it is not a recoverable pipeline fault.
193	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
194	            pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
195	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
196	            prov.append(_prov(
197	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
198	            ))
199	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
200	                raise
201	            break
202	
203	    # phase_1_ready only if all four stages completed (no stubs/failures in statuses)
204	    all_complete = all(s["status"] == "completed" for s in pipeline_statuses)
205	
206	    return {
207	        **accumulated_update,
208	        "phase_1_ready": all_complete,
209	        "eda_agent_statuses": pipeline_statuses,
210	        "failure_events": failures,
211	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("data_pipeline_node", run_id,
212	                                         all_complete=all_complete)],
213	    }
214	
215	
216	# ── Exploratory phase node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
217	
218	
219	def exploratory_phase_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Calls P1-P5 exploratory analysis sequentially. Catches NotImplementedError
222	    per agent and records stub_pending status.
223	
224	    PARALLELIZATION NOTE: When P1-P5 implementations are live, replace this node
225	    with a Send()-based fan-out. See module docstring for the pattern.
226	
227	    P1-P5 are PROTECTED agents — they are called via __call__() which dispatches
228	    to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]. Each returns a partial state
229	    update dict with eda_artifacts appended.
230	
231	    Gate 2 is NOT required here — this is exploratory phase. Each agent internally
232	    enforces the EXP_ artifact naming convention and no p-value decision language.
233	    """
234	    run_id = state["run_id"]
235	    agent_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
236	    all_eda_artifacts: list[str] = []
237	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
238	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
239	
240	    research_agents = [
241	        (_p1, "p1-strm-nlp"),
242	        (_p2, "p2-control-topology"),
243	        (_p3, "p3-regulatory-convergence"),
244	        (_p4, "p4-risk-blindspot"),
245	        (_p5, "p5-ai-governance"),
246	    ]
247	
248	    for agent, agent_id in research_agents:
249	        try:
250	            # __call__ dispatches to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]
251	            agent_update = agent(state)
252	            artifacts = agent_update.get("eda_artifacts", [])
253	            all_eda_artifacts.extend(artifacts)
254	            agent_statuses.append({
255	                "agent_id": agent_id,
256	                "status": "completed",
257	                "artifact_count": len(artifacts),
258	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
259	                "run_id": run_id,
260	            })
261	            prov.append(_prov(f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}", run_id,
262	                               artifact_count=len(artifacts)))
263	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
264	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
265	                # Real bug from an implemented agent — record as failed, not stub (#202).
266	                agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
267	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
268	                prov.append(_prov(
269	                    f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
270	                ))
271	                continue
272	            agent_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
273	            prov.append(_prov(
274	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
275	                run_id,
276	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
277	            ))
278	        except Exception as exc:
279	            # P1-P5 are independent — one agent's runtime error is recorded as
280	            # 'failed' and the loop continues so the other agents' artifacts are
281	            # preserved, instead of crashing the whole exploratory subgraph (#148).
282	            # A governance violation must propagate, not be swallowed.
283	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
284	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
285	                raise
286	            agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
287	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
288	            prov.append(_prov(
289	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
290	            ))
291	
292	    all_stubs = all(s["status"] == "stub_pending" for s in agent_statuses)
293	    any_complete = any(s["status"] == "completed" for s in agent_statuses)
294	    exploratory_complete = any_complete and not all_stubs
295	
296	    # exploratory_complete = True only if at least one agent returned real results.
297	    # In Phase 1 dry-run (all stubs), stays False — Gate 2 checklist will surface this.
298	    return {
299	        "phase": "phase_1",
300	        "eda_artifacts": all_eda_artifacts,
301	        "eda_agent_statuses": agent_statuses,
302	        "failure_events": failures,
303	        "exploratory_complete": exploratory_complete,
304	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("exploratory_phase_node", run_id,
305	                                          exploratory_complete=exploratory_complete)],
306	    }
307	
308	
309	# ── Hypothesis formalization node ─────────────────────────────────────────────
310	
311	
312	def hypothesis_formalize_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
313	    """
314	    Calls HypothesisFormalizerAgent to produce the pre-registered hypothesis set.
315	
316	    Runs after exploratory analysis completes. Output populates state["hypothesis_set"]
317	    with FormalHypothesis dicts. This is the set that gets locked at Gate 2.
318	
319	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent is PROTECTED. Its run_exploratory() builds hypotheses
320	    from EDA outputs and HARKing validation stub. Output is the Gate 2 input.
321	    """
322	    run_id = state["run_id"]
323	    agent_id = "hypothesis-formalizer"
324	
325	    try:
326	        agent_update = _hypothesis_formalizer(state)
327	        hypothesis_count = len(agent_update.get("hypothesis_set", []))
328	        return {
329	            **agent_update,
330	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization", run_id,
331	                                       hypothesis_count=hypothesis_count)],
332	        }
333	    except NotImplementedError as exc:
334	        # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError;
335	        # once IMPLEMENTED it is a real bug, not a stub (#237).
336	        if getattr(_hypothesis_formalizer, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
337	            return {
338	                "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
339	                "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
340	                "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
341	                                          error=repr(exc))],
342	            }
343	        return {
344	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_stub_status(agent_id, run_id)],
345	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_stub", run_id,
346	                                       note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 pending")],
347	        }
348	    except Exception as exc:
349	        # Isolate a real runtime error: record it instead of crashing the graph,
350	        # and emit a failure_event for the monitor (#35). SAP violations propagate.
351	        from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
352	        if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
353	            raise
354	        return {
355	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
356	            "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
357	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
358	                                      error=repr(exc))],
359	        }
360	
361	
362	# ── Confirmatory entry stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
363	
364	
365	def confirmatory_entry_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
366	    """
367	    Entry point for Phase 2 confirmatory analysis subgraph.
368	
369	    Gate 2 approval is required before this node runs (enforced by
370	    route_after_gate_2 in orchestrator.py). The confirmatory subgraph
371	    (statistical-analyst + P1-P5 confirmatory runs) is Phase 2 mechanism work.
372	    """
373	    return {
374	        "phase": "phase_2",
375	        "prov_activities": [
376	            _prov(
377	                "confirmatory_entry",
378	                state["run_id"],
379	                note="Phase 2 confirmatory subgraph — not yet implemented",
380	            )
381	        ],
382	    }

1	# Pre-registration Ledger
2	
3	**Type:** Append-only. No entry is ever modified or deleted after commitment.
4	**Backing:** This file is committed to the `pre-registration` protected branch. The branch has no force-push and no history-rewriting permitted.
5	**Timestamps:** Every entry requiring a timestamp uses RFC 3161 via a trusted timestamp authority. The `.tsr` file is stored in `protocol/registration/` alongside this document.
6	
7	This ledger is the single document a journal reviewer, audit committee, or Adversarial Protocol arbiter would request first. It must be complete, current, and honest — including gate rejections, protocol revisions, and anything that did not go according to plan.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Entry Format
12	
13	```
14	---
15	type: [pre-registration | gate-decision | protocol-revision | incident-ref | sap-amendment]
16	entry_id: LEDGER-NNNN
17	date: YYYY-MM-DD
18	author: [human name | orchestrator]
19	rfc3161_tsr: protocol/registration/LEDGER-NNNN.tsr
20	---
21	
22	### Summary
23	[One paragraph. What happened. What was decided. Why.]
24	
25	### Details
26	[Required fields per entry type — see below.]
27	
28	### Dissent
29	[Null if none. Text if present. Required field — never omit.]
30	```
31	
32	---
33	
34	## Entry Types and Required Fields
35	
36	### `pre-registration`
37	Records a locked protocol artifact being committed to the pre-registration branch.
38	
39	Required fields:
40	- `artifact`: filename and path
41	- `git_commit_sha`: commit SHA on pre-registration branch
42	- `rfc3161_tsr`: path to timestamp file
43	- `content_hash`: SHA-256 of the artifact at time of registration
44	- `registered_by`: human reviewer name
45	
46	### `gate-decision`
47	Records a human approval gate decision.
48	
49	Required fields:
50	- `gate_id`: gate-1 through gate-5
51	- `outcome`: approved | rejected | deferred
52	- `reviewer`: human reviewer name
53	- `rationale`: minimum 20 words describing the specific basis for the decision
54	- `dissent`: null | text
55	- `run_manifest_ref`: run_id of the pipeline run triggering the gate
